questions.sentence: Building scalable Vue.js applications requires a clear strategy for communication between components.
The most fundamental pattern is 'Props down, Events up'.
If a child component needs to trigger a change or notify the parent, it 'Emits' an event using the '$emit' function.
This keeps the data flow predictable and follows the principle of 'one-way data flow'.

Understanding these patterns is crucial for managing application state without creating 'prop drilling'—the anti-pattern of passing props through many layers of components that don't need the data themselves.
